title: I2C sensor node on Arduino
prompt: I'm adding an I2C sensor module (say a temp/humidity breakout) to my Arduino Uno (ATmega328). Wire it to the I2C pins with the required pull-up resistors to the 5 V rail, give the module power and ground, and account for its supply current. Firmware will talk to it over I2C. For the bench check the idle bus should sit high at the rail through the pull-ups.

abstraction.reason: the I2C sensor's data path is out of analog scope (M8); only its electrical envelope (supply draw + open-drain pull-ups) is modelled, per the #105 generic_load + I2C stub convention
